Faqs.Question: I'm interested in try dive
Faqs.Answer: Try Dives are £25.  We meet at the Waterside Centre and head over to the swimming pool shortly afterwards where you will spend between 30 to 60 minutes in the pool with an experienced qualified instructor and experience the thrill of breathing under water. Send us an email to arrange.

Faqs.Question: How much is an introductory course and when does the course run?
Faqs.Answer:
The initial qualification level is BSAC Ocean Diver. There is a Club joining fee which includes the training manuals and use of club equipment while training. There is also BSAC membership fee and Club membership. Please see our current Membership Prices.

There are 6 classroom lessons, 5 sheltered water (pool) lessons and 4 open water lessons, plus a theory and swimming assessment. Training can be organised in different ways, but we typically run 1 classroom or pool lesson a week and complete the open water lessons over 1 weekend, making 12 weeks in total. As soon as you join the club we will team you up with an instructor and commence training.

We also offer try dives for £25, and should you decide to join we will refund the try dive fee.

Faqs.Question: My child would like to learn to dive.  Can you help?
Faqs.Answer: We offer training to anyone aged 12 or over. Anyone under 18 must be accompanied by a parent or guardian.

Faqs.Question: Is it possible to arrange training before I go on holiday
Faqs.Answer:
The initial qualification level is BSAC Ocean Diver. There are 6 classroom lessons, 5 sheltered water (pool) lessons and 4 open water lessons, plus a theory and swimming assessment. Training can be organised in different ways, but we typically run 1 classroom or pool lesson a week and complete the open water lessons over 1 weekend, making 12 weeks in total.

Training in a club environment will cost significantly less than a commercially run diving school, but it will take more time. If you want to be qualified as quickly as possible, we would suggest you train with a diving school. Find a BSAC center

Faqs.Question: I'm going diving soon and wonder if you run a refresher session
Faqs.Answer: Sure, if you haven't been diving for a while and need to refresh your scuba skills we're happy to help. A refresher session is £25.00. We meet at the Waterside Centre and head over to the swimming pool shortly afterwards. Send us an email to arrange.

Faqs.Question: I'd like to get a sub aqua qualification to become stunt performer.
Faqs.Answer:
You need to achieve one of the qualifications below:

  • CMAS Three Star or equivalent which includes
  • BSAC Advanced Diver
  • PADI Dive Master
  • HSE Scuba

We can train you and training in a club environment will cost significantly less than a commercially run diving school, but it will take more time. If you want to be qualified as quickly as possible, we would suggest you train with diving school. Find a BSAC center
